verses.latin_vulgate: In illo tempore, ait Dominus,  ejicient ossa regum Juda, et ossa principum ejus,  et ossa sacerdotum, et ossa prophetarum,  et ossa eorum qui habitaverunt Jerusalem,  de sepulchris suis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּעֵ֣ת הַהִ֣יא נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֡ה (ויציאו) [יוֹצִ֣יאוּ] אֶת־עַצְמ֣וֹת מַלְכֵי־יְהוּדָ֣ה וְאֶת־עַצְמוֹת־שָׂרָיו֩ וְאֶת־עַצְמ֨וֹת הַכֹּהֲנִ֜ים וְאֵ֣ת ׀ עַצְמ֣וֹת הַנְּבִיאִ֗ים וְאֵ֛ת עַצְמ֥וֹת יוֹשְׁבֵֽי־יְרוּשָׁלָ֖͏ִם מִקִּבְרֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: At that time, saith the Lord, they shall cast out the bones of the kings of Juda, and the bones of the princes thereof, and the bones of the priests, and the bones of the prophets, and the bones of the inhabitants of Jerusalem, out of their graves.
verses.finnish_biblia: Siihen aikaan, sanoo Herra, pitää Juudan kuningasten luut, ja hänen päämiestensä luut, pappein luut, ja prophetain luut, ja Jerusalemissa asuvaisten luut, heitettämän ulos heidän haudoistansa
verses.king_james: At that time, saith the Lord, they shall bring out the bones of the kings of Judah, and the bones of his princes, and the bones of the priests, and the bones of the prophets, and the bones of the inhabitants of Jerusalem, out of their graves:

verses.latin_vulgate: et expandent ea ad solem, et lunam,  et omnem militiam cæli,  quæ dilexerunt, et quibus servierunt,  et post quæ ambulaverunt,  et quæ quæsierunt, et adoraverunt.  Non colligentur, et non sepelientur :  in sterquilinium super faciem terræ erunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּשְׁטָחוּם֩ לַשֶּׁ֨מֶשׁ וְלַיָּרֵ֜חַ וּלְכֹ֣ל ׀ צְבָ֣א הַשָּׁמַ֗יִם אֲשֶׁ֨ר אֲהֵב֜וּם וַאֲשֶׁ֤ר עֲבָדוּם֙ וַֽאֲשֶׁר֙ הָלְכ֣וּ אַחֲרֵיהֶ֔ם וַאֲשֶׁ֣ר דְּרָשׁ֔וּם וַאֲשֶׁ֥ר הִֽשְׁתַּחֲו֖וּ לָהֶ֑ם לֹ֤א יֵאָֽסְפוּ֙ וְלֹ֣א יִקָּבֵ֔רוּ לְדֹ֛מֶן עַל־פְּנֵ֥י הָאֲדָמָ֖ה יִהְיֽוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they shall spread them abroad to the sun, and the moon, and all the host of heaven, whom they have loved, and whom they have served, and after whom have walked, and whom they have sought, and adored: they shall not be gathered, and they shall not be buried: they shall be as dung upon the face of the earth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ne pitää hajoitettaman auringon, kuun ja kaiken taivaan sotajoukon alle, joita he rakastaneet, palvelleet, seuranneet, etsineet ja rukoilleet ovat; ei he pidä enään koottaman eikä haudattaman, vaan pitää oleman lokana maassa.
verses.king_james: And they shall spread them before the sun, and the moon, and all the host of heaven, whom they have loved, and whom they have served, and after whom they have walked, and whom they have sought, and whom they have worshipped: they shall not be gathered, nor be buried; they shall be for dung upon the face of the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et eligent magis mortem quam vitam,  omnes qui residui fuerint de cognatione hac pessima,  in universis locis quæ derelicta sunt,  ad quæ ejeci eos, dicit Dominus exercituum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנִבְחַ֥ר מָ֙וֶת֙ מֵחַיִּ֔ים לְכֹ֗ל הַשְּׁאֵרִית֙ הַנִּשְׁאָרִ֔ים מִן־הַמִּשְׁפָּחָ֥ה הָרָעָ֖ה הַזֹּ֑את בְּכׇל־הַמְּקֹמ֤וֹת הַנִּשְׁאָרִים֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר הִדַּחְתִּ֣ים שָׁ֔ם נְאֻ֖ם יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָאֽוֹת׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And death shall be chosen rather than life by all that shall remain of this wicked kindred in all places, which are left, to which I have cast them out, saith the Lord of hosts.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kaikki jääneet tästä pahasta kansasta, missä ikänä paikassa he ovat, johon minä heitä ajanut olen, pitää paremmin tahtoman olla kuolleina kuin elävinä, sanoo Herra Zebaot.
verses.king_james: And death shall be chosen rather than life by all the residue of them that remain of this evil family, which remain in all the places whither I have driven them, saith the Lord of hosts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dices ad eos :   Hæc dicit Dominus :  Numquid qui cadit non resurget ?  et qui aversus est non revertetur ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָמַרְתָּ֣ אֲלֵיהֶ֗ם כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה הֲיִפְּל֖וּ וְלֹ֣א יָק֑וּמוּ אִם־יָשׁ֖וּב וְלֹ֥א יָשֽׁוּב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And thou shalt say to them: Thus saith the Lord: Shall not he that falleth, rise again? and he that is turned away, shall he not turn again?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sinun pitää heille sanoman: näin sanoo Herra: kussa on joku, jos hän lankee, joka ei jälleen mielellänsä nouse? kussa on joku, joka käy eksyksissä, eikö hän mielellänsä jälleen oikialle tielle tule?
verses.king_james: Moreover thou shalt say unto them, Thus saith the Lord; Shall they fall, and not arise? shall he turn away, and not return?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare ergo aversus est populus iste in Jerusalem  aversione contentiosa ?  Apprehenderunt mendacium,  et noluerunt reverti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַדּ֨וּעַ שׁוֹבְבָ֜ה הָעָ֥ם הַזֶּ֛ה יְרוּשָׁלַ֖͏ִם מְשֻׁבָ֣ה נִצַּ֑חַת הֶחֱזִ֙יקוּ֙ בַּתַּרְמִ֔ת מֵאֲנ֖וּ לָשֽׁוּב׃
verses.douay_rheims: Why then is this people in Jerusalem turned away with a stubborn revolting? they have laid hold on lying, and have refused to return.
verses.finnish_biblia: Miksi siis tämä Jerusalemin kansa tahtoo käydä eksyksissä palajamatta? ja riippuvat niin kovin valheessa, ettei he millään tavalla anna kääntää itseänsä pois?
verses.king_james: Why then is this people of Jerusalem slidden back by a perpetual backsliding? they hold fast deceit, they refuse to return.

verses.latin_vulgate: Attendi, et auscultavi :  nemo quod bonum est loquitur ;  nullus est qui agat pœnitentiam super peccato suo,  dicens : Quid feci ?  Omnes conversi sunt ad cursum suum,  quasi equus impetu vadens ad prælium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִקְשַׁ֤בְתִּי וָֽאֶשְׁמָע֙ לוֹא־כֵ֣ן יְדַבֵּ֔רוּ אֵ֣ין אִ֗ישׁ נִחָם֙ עַל־רָ֣עָת֔וֹ לֵאמֹ֖ר מֶ֣ה עָשִׂ֑יתִי כֻּלֹּ֗ה שָׁ֚ב (במרצותם) [בִּמְר֣וּצָתָ֔ם] כְּס֥וּס שׁוֹטֵ֖ף בַּמִּלְחָמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: I attended, and hearkened; no man speaketh what is good, there is none that doth penance for his sin, saying: What have I done? They are all turned to their own course, as a horse rushing to the battle.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä havaitsen ja kuulen, ettei he oikein opeta; ei ole yhtään, joka katuu pahuuttansa ja sanoo: mitä minä tein? He juoksevat kaikki juoksuansa niinkuin ankara hevonen sodassa.
verses.king_james: I hearkened and heard, but they spake not aright: no man repented him of his wickedness, saying, What have I done? every one turned to his course, as the horse rusheth into the battle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Milvus in cælo cognovit tempus suum :  turtur, et hirundo, et ciconia custodierunt tempus adventus sui :  populus autem meus non cognovit judicium Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־חֲסִידָ֣ה בַשָּׁמַ֗יִם יָֽדְעָה֙ מוֹעֲדֶ֔יהָ וְתֹ֤ר (וסוס) [וְסִיס֙] וְעָג֔וּר שָׁמְר֖וּ אֶת־עֵ֣ת בֹּאָ֑נָה וְעַמִּ֕י לֹ֣א יָדְע֔וּ אֵ֖ת מִשְׁפַּ֥ט יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The kite in the air hath known her time: the turtle, and the swallow, and the stork have observed the time of their coming: but my people have not known the judgment of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Haikara taivaan alla tietää aikansa, mettinen, kurki ja pääskynen huomaitsevat aikansa, kuin heidän pitää tuleman jälleen; vaan minun kansani ei tiedä Herran oikeutta.
verses.king_james: Yea, the stork in the heaven knoweth her appointed times; and the turtle and the crane and the swallow observe the time of their coming; but my people know not the judgment of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo dicitis : Sapientes nos sumus,  et lex Domini nobiscum est ?  vere mendacium operatus est stylus mendax scribarum !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵיכָ֤ה תֹֽאמְרוּ֙ חֲכָמִ֣ים אֲנַ֔חְנוּ וְתוֹרַ֥ת יְהֹוָ֖ה אִתָּ֑נוּ אָכֵן֙ הִנֵּ֣ה לַשֶּׁ֣קֶר עָשָׂ֔ה עֵ֖ט שֶׁ֥קֶר סֹפְרִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: How do you say: We are wise, and the law of the Lord is with us? Indeed the lying pens of the scribes hath wrought falsehood.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuinka te siis taidatte sanoa: me olemme viisaat, ja meillä on Herran laki? Katso, se on totisesti kuitenkin valhe, mitä kirjanoppineet kirjoittavat.
verses.king_james: How do ye say, We are wise, and the law of the Lord is with us? Lo, certainly in vain made he it; the pen of the scribes is in vain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Confusi sunt sapientes ;  perterriti et capti sunt :  verbum enim Domini projecerunt,  et sapientia nulla est in eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֹבִ֣שׁוּ חֲכָמִ֔ים חַ֖תּוּ וַיִּלָּכֵ֑דוּ הִנֵּ֤ה בִדְבַר־יְהֹוָה֙ מָאָ֔סוּ וְחׇכְמַ֥ת מֶ֖ה לָהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: The wise men are confounded, they are dismayed, and taken: for they have cast away the word of the Lord, and there is no wisdom in them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sentähden pitää senkaltaiset opettajat häväistämän, peljätettämän ja otettaman kiinni: katso, he ovat hyljänneet Herran sanan, mitä viisautta pitäis heillä oleman?
verses.king_james: The wise men are ashamed, they are dismayed and taken: lo, they have rejected the word of the Lord; and what wisdom is in them?

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea dabo mulieres eorum exteris,  agros eorum hæredibus,  quia a minimo usque ad maximum  omnes avaritiam sequuntur :  a propheta usque ad sacerdotem  cuncti faciunt mendacium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵן֩ אֶתֵּ֨ן אֶת־נְשֵׁיהֶ֜ם לַאֲחֵרִ֗ים שְׂדֽוֹתֵיהֶם֙ לְי֣וֹרְשִׁ֔ים כִּ֤י מִקָּטֹן֙ וְעַד־גָּד֔וֹל כֻּלֹּ֖ה בֹּצֵ֣עַ בָּ֑צַע מִנָּבִיא֙ וְעַד־כֹּהֵ֔ן כֻּלֹּ֖ה עֹ֥שֶׂה שָּֽׁקֶר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Therefore will I give their women to strangers, their fields to others for an inheritance: because from the least even to the greatest all follow covetousness: from the prophet even to the priest all deal deceitfully.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sentähden annan minä heidän emäntänsä muukalaisille ja heidän peltonsa niille, jotka heitä karkoittavat; sillä he ovat kaikki ahneudelta voitetut, pienimmästä niin suurimpaan, papit ja prophetat opettavat kaikki valhetta.
verses.king_james: Therefore will I give their wives unto others, and their fields to them that shall inherit them: for every one from the least even unto the greatest is given to covetousness, from the prophet even unto the priest every one dealeth falsely.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et sanabant contritionem filiæ populi mei ad ignominiam,  dicentes : Pax, pax !  cum non esset pax.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְרַפּ֞וּ אֶת־שֶׁ֤בֶר בַּת־עַמִּי֙ עַל־נְקַלָּ֔ה לֵאמֹ֖ר שָׁל֣וֹם ׀ שָׁל֑וֹם וְאֵ֖ין שָׁלֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they healed the breach of the daughter of my people disgracefully, saying: Peace, peace: when there was no peace.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja rohkaisevat minun kansani tytärtä onnettomuudessansa, sitä turhana pitämään, ja sanovat: rauha, rauha, ja rauhaa ei olekaan.
verses.king_james: For they have healed the hurt of the daughter of my people slightly, saying, Peace, peace; when there is no peace.

verses.latin_vulgate: Confusi sunt, quia abominationem fecerunt :  quinimmo confusione non sunt confusi,  et erubescere nescierunt.  Idcirco cadent inter corruentes :  in tempore visitationis suæ corruent, dicit Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֹבִ֕שׁוּ כִּ֥י תוֹעֵבָ֖ה עָשׂ֑וּ גַּם־בּ֣וֹשׁ לֹֽא־יֵבֹ֗שׁוּ וְהִכָּלֵם֙ לֹ֣א יָדָ֔עוּ לָכֵ֞ן יִפְּל֣וּ בַנֹּפְלִ֗ים בְּעֵ֧ת פְּקֻדָּתָ֛ם יִכָּשְׁל֖וּ אָמַ֥ר יְהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: They are confounded, because they have committed abomination: yea rather they are not confounded with confusion, and they have not known how to blush: therefore shall they fall among them that fall; in the time of their visitation they shall fall, saith the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sentähden pitää heidän häpiään tuleman, että he kauhistuksen tekevät; ja vaikka he tahtovat ilman häväistystä olla, eikä taida hävetä, kuitenkin pitää heidän lankeeman toinen toisensa päälle, ja kuin minä heitä kuritan, silloin pitää heidän lankeeman, sanoo Herra.
verses.king_james: Were they ashamed when they had committed abomination? nay, they were not at all ashamed, neither could they blush: therefore shall they fall among them that fall: in the time of their visitation they shall be cast down, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Congregans congregabo eos, ait Dominus.  Non est uva in vitibus, et non sunt ficus in ficulnea :  folium defluxit,  et dedi eis quæ prætergressa sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָסֹ֥ף אֲסִיפֵ֖ם נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֑ה אֵין֩ עֲנָבִ֨ים בַּגֶּ֜פֶן וְאֵ֧ין תְּאֵנִ֣ים בַּתְּאֵנָ֗ה וְהֶעָלֶה֙ נָבֵ֔ל וָאֶתֵּ֥ן לָהֶ֖ם יַעַבְרֽוּם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Gathering I will gather them together, saith the Lord, there is no grape on the vines, and there are no figs on the fig tree, the leaf is fallen: and I have given them the things that are passed away.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä tahdon heitä niin poimia, sanoo Herra, ettei yhtäkään viinimarjaa pidä viinapuussa oleman, eikä yhtäkään fiikunapuussa, lehdet pitää variseman; ja se, minkä minä heille antanut olen, pitää heiltä otettaman pois.
verses.king_james: I will surely consume them, saith the Lord: there shall be no grapes on the vine, nor figs on the fig tree, and the leaf shall fade; and the things that I have given them shall pass away from them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare sedemus ?  convenite, et ingrediamur civitatem munitam,  et sileamus ibi :  quia Dominus Deus noster silere nos fecit,  et potum dedit nobis aquam fellis :  peccavimus enim Domino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־מָה֙ אֲנַ֣חְנוּ יֹֽשְׁבִ֔ים הֵאָסְפ֗וּ וְנָב֛וֹא אֶל־עָרֵ֥י הַמִּבְצָ֖ר וְנִדְּמָה־שָּׁ֑ם כִּי֩ יְהֹוָ֨ה אֱלֹהֵ֤ינוּ הֲדִמָּ֙נוּ֙ וַיַּשְׁקֵ֣נוּ מֵי־רֹ֔אשׁ כִּ֥י חָטָ֖אנוּ לַיהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Why do we sit still? assemble yourselves, and let us enter into the fenced city, and let us be silent there: for the Lord our God hath put us to silence, and hath given us water of gall to drink: for we have sinned against the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Miksi me niin alallamme istumme? Kootkaat teitänne, käykäämme vahvoihin kaupunkeihin ja odottakaamme niissä apua: sillä Herra meidän Jumalamme on meidän käskenyt odottaa, ja juottanut meitä katkeralla siemenellä, että me niin teimme Herraa vastaan syntiä.
verses.king_james: Why do we sit still? assemble yourselves, and let us enter into the defenced cities, and let us be silent there: for the Lord our God hath put us to silence, and given us water of gall to drink, because we have sinned against the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exspectavimus pacem, et non erat bonum :  tempus medelæ, et ecce formido.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קַוֵּ֥ה לְשָׁל֖וֹם וְאֵ֣ין ט֑וֹב לְעֵ֥ת מַרְפֵּ֖ה וְהִנֵּ֥ה בְעָתָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: We looked for peace and no good came: for a time of healing, and behold fear.
verses.finnish_biblia: Odota rauhaa ja terveyden aikaa, ehkei mitään hyvää ole käsissä, ja että te parannettaisiin, ehkei muuta ole kuin hämmästys.
verses.king_james: We looked for peace, but no good came; and for a time of health, and behold trouble!

verses.latin_vulgate: A Dan auditus est fremitus equorum ejus ;  a voce hinnituum pugnatorum ejus commota est omnis terra :  et venerunt, et devoraverunt terram et plenitudinem ejus ;  urbem et habitatores ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִדָּ֤ן נִשְׁמַע֙ נַחְרַ֣ת סוּסָ֔יו מִקּוֹל֙ מִצְהֲל֣וֹת אַבִּירָ֔יו רָעֲשָׁ֖ה כׇּל־הָאָ֑רֶץ וַיָּב֗וֹאוּ וַיֹּֽאכְלוּ֙ אֶ֣רֶץ וּמְלוֹאָ֔הּ עִ֖יר וְיֹ֥שְׁבֵי בָֽהּ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: The snorting of his horses was heard from Dan, all the land was moved at the sound of the neighing of his warriors: and they came and devoured the land, and all that was in it: the city and its inhabitants.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jo nyt kuuluu hamasta Danista hevosten hirnuminen, ja heidän orhiinsa korskuvat, niin että kaikki maa järisee. He tulevat ja syövät maan, ja kaikki mitä siinä on, kaupungin ja sen asuvaiset.
verses.king_james: The snorting of his horses was heard from Dan: the whole land trembled at the sound of the neighing of his strong ones; for they are come, and have devoured the land, and all that is in it; the city, and those that dwell therein.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia ecce ego mittam vobis serpentes regulos,  quibus non est incantatio :  et mordebunt vos, ait Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי֩ הִנְנִ֨י מְשַׁלֵּ֜חַ בָּכֶ֗ם נְחָשִׁים֙ צִפְעֹנִ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר אֵין־לָהֶ֖ם לָ֑חַשׁ וְנִשְּׁכ֥וּ אֶתְכֶ֖ם נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: For behold I will send among you serpents, basilisks, against which there is no charm: and they shall bite you, saith the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä katso, minä lähetän kärmeet ja basiliskit teidän sekaanne, joita ei auta lumoomaan; vaan niiden pitää teitä pistämän, sanoo Herra.
verses.king_james: For, behold, I will send serpents, cockatrices, among you, which will not be charmed, and they shall bite you, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dolor meus super dolorem,  in me cor meum mœrens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַבְלִ֥יגִיתִ֖י עֲלֵ֣י יָג֑וֹן עָלַ֖י לִבִּ֥י דַוָּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: My sorrow is above sorrow, my heart mourneth within me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun virvoitukseni on murheella sekoitettu, ja minun sydämeni on nääntynyt.
verses.king_james: When I would comfort myself against sorrow, my heart is faint in me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce vox clamoris filiæ populi mei de terra longinqua :  Numquid Dominus non est in Sion ?  aut rex ejus non est in ea ?  Quare ergo me ad iracundiam concitaverunt in sculptilibus suis,  et in vanitatibus alienis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּה־ק֞וֹל שַֽׁוְעַ֣ת בַּת־עַמִּ֗י מֵאֶ֙רֶץ֙ מַרְחַקִּ֔ים הַֽיהֹוָה֙ אֵ֣ין בְּצִיּ֔וֹן אִם־מַלְכָּ֖הּ אֵ֣ין בָּ֑הּ מַדּ֗וּעַ הִכְעִס֛וּנִי בִּפְסִלֵיהֶ֖ם בְּהַבְלֵ֥י נֵכָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Behold the voice of the daughter of my people from a far country: Is not the Lord in Sion, or is not her king in her? why then have they provoked me to wrath with their idols, and strange vanities?
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, minun kansani tyttären pitää vieraalta maalta huutaman: eikö Herra tahdo enään olla Zionissa? eli eikö hänellä enään ole kuningasta? Miksi he vihoittivat minun kuvillansa ja turhalla vieraan jumalan palveluksella?
verses.king_james: Behold the voice of the cry of the daughter of my people because of them that dwell in a far country: Is not the Lord in Zion? is not her king in her? Why have they provoked me to anger with their graven images, and with strange vanities?

verses.latin_vulgate: Transiit messis, finita est æstas,  et nos salvati non sumus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָבַ֥ר קָצִ֖יר כָּ֣לָה קָ֑יִץ וַאֲנַ֖חְנוּ ל֥וֹא נוֹשָֽׁעְנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The harvest is past, the summer is ended, and we are not saved.
verses.finnish_biblia: Elonaika on mennyt edes, suvi on loppunut, ja ei meille ei ole apua tullut.
verses.king_james: The harvest is past, the summer is ended, and we are not saved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Super contritione filiæ populi mei contritus sum, et contristatus :  stupor obtinuit me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־שֶׁ֥בֶר בַּת־עַמִּ֖י הׇשְׁבָּ֑רְתִּי קָדַ֕רְתִּי שַׁמָּ֖ה הֶחֱזִקָֽתְנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: For the affliction of the daughter of my people I am afflicted, and made sorrowful, astonishment hath taken hold on me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä suren suuresti, että minun kansani tytär, niin turmeltu on; minä surkuttelen ja olen hämmästyksissä.
verses.king_james: For the hurt of the daughter of my people am I hurt; I am black; astonishment hath taken hold on me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid resina non est in Galaad ?  aut medicus non est ibi ?  quare igitur non est obducta cicatrix filiæ populi mei ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַצֳּרִי֙ אֵ֣ין בְּגִלְעָ֔ד אִם־רֹפֵ֖א אֵ֣ין שָׁ֑ם כִּ֗י מַדּ֙וּעַ֙ לֹ֣א עָֽלְתָ֔ה אֲרֻכַ֖ת בַּת־עַמִּֽי׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Is there no balm in Galaad? or is there no physician there? Why then is not the wound of the daughter of my people closed?
verses.finnish_biblia: Eikö Gileadissa ole voidetta, eli eikö siellä ole parantajaa? miksi ei minun kansani tytär ole terveeksi tehty?
verses.king_james: Is there no balm in Gilead; is there no physician there? why then is not the health of the daughter of my people recovered?
